title: Cashew nut yields
subtitle: Yields are measured in tonnes per hectare.
caption:
Cashew nut yields, 1980 to 2024
Yields are measured in tonnes per hectare.

The line chart tracks agricultural output for four countries from 1980 through 2024, with the vertical axis measuring yields in tonnes per hectare ranging from 0 to 7. Peru displays the highest productivity levels for the majority of the timeline, with its brown line consistently occupying the upper portion of the graph. Between 1990 and 2015, Peru’s yields fluctuate mostly between 4 and 7 tonnes per hectare, reaching a peak near 6.8 tonnes around 2005 before trending downward to roughly 5.3 tonnes by 2024. Mexico, represented by the blue line, shows extreme volatility early in the period, spiking above 6 tonnes in 1982 before falling sharply and remaining below 3 tonnes for most subsequent years, ending near 0.8 tonnes. China’s red line stays near the bottom of the scale, hovering between 0.2 and 1.5 tonnes per hectare throughout the forty-four years. Mali’s green line begins around 1992 at approximately 1.3 tonnes, rises gradually to over 4 tonnes by 2018, and then drops steeply to just above 1 tonne by 2024. The data shows a wide divergence in agricultural efficiency, with Peru maintaining yields several times higher than China and Mexico across the observed period.
